questions.question: The main purpose of constraints is to ______.
options: limit what data is allowed in a table
define how other tables reference a given table
restrict which queries can be used to modify a table

questions.question: Constraints can be used on columns and tables.
If records in a table cannot have a NULL value for a column, then that column should\nhave what type of constraint?
Suppose the table Products has the column Price which holds amount of money each\nproduct costs. A product must have a price. What constraint is necessary for this column?
The INDEX constraint creates an index of all the columns in a table.
Which constraint is used to ensure all values of a column meet a certain condition?
In SQL Server, what type of join is performed by the following query: SELECT * FROM\nStudent AS s JOIN Enrollment AS e ON s.Id = c.StudentId

Suppose we have two tables, Student and Enrollment. Which type of join would return all\nthe Student records and the matched Enrollment records, assuming Student is the left\ntable and Enrollment is the right table?
The WITH clause can be used to join tables based on a condition.
UNION ALL is different from UNION because ______.
options: UNION ALL selects all the records in both tables
UNION ALL can be used with any number of tables

questions.question:
Look at the following SQL query:
SELECT p.Name
FROM Person AS p
UNION
SELECT e.ID, e.EmployeeName
FROM Employee AS e;
What is wrong with this query?
options: Both SELECT statements do not select the same number of columns.
The SELECT statements do not query from the same table.
UNION ALL should be used instead of UNION.

questions.question: Each SELECT statement in a UNION must have the same number of columns.
Each SELECT statement in a UNION can have different types of columns.
Which command is used to combine the results of two or more SELECT statements?
Which command is used to combine the distinct results of two or more SELECT\nstatements?
Microsoft SQL Server uses the ______ dialect of SQL, while Oracle uses ______.
What is a schema in Microsoft SQL Server?
options: The definition of a table in a database
The definition of a database in a server

questions.question: In SQL Server, a ______ is a set of tables that contains a database’s definition.
A foreign key constraint can be added to a table after the table is created.
Which query correctly adds a foreign key to an existing table?
options: ALTER TABLE Person ADD FOREIGN KEY (ID) REFERENCES Person(ID);
UPDATE TABLE Person ADD FOREIGN KEY (ID) REFERENCES\nPerson(ID);
ALTER TABLE Person FOREIGN KEY (ID) REFERENCES Person(ID);
UPDATE TABLE Person FOREIGN KEY (ID) REFERENCES Person(ID);

questions.question: Which of the following data type categories does not exist in all SQL databases?
What is the difference between the int and bigint types in Transact-SQL?
options: Bigint can hold decimals, int can only hold integers
Bigint takes up 2 bytes of space, int takes up 4 bytes
Bigint takes up 8 bytes of space, int takes up 2 bytes
Bigint takes up 8 bytes of space, int takes up 4 bytes

questions.question: Which of the following Transact-SQL data types would be best suited for storing a\nperson’s birthday (storing only the day when the person was born)?
Which of the following Transact-SQL data types would be best suited for storing the\nprice of an item?
All SQL databases have a data type for storing dates and times.
Preparing SQL code that can be saved to be executed in the future requires creating a(n)\n______.
Stored procedures cannot take user defined parameters, a parameterized query must be\nused instead.
Stored procedures cannot ______.
options: return a status value to a calling procedure
all the above can be done with stored procedures

questions.question: Which of the following is a disadvantage of stored procedures?
options: Stored procedures cannot be easily ported to another RDBMS
Stored procedures allow for SQL injection from user input
Stored procedures are not efficient for processing large queries

questions.question: Which command is used to run a stored procedure in SQL Server?
Which aggregate function is used to get the largest value of the selected column?
Which aggregate function is used to get the number of rows returned by a SELECT\nstatement?
What is the difference between aggregate and scalar functions?
options: Aggregate functions take many values while scalar functions take one value.
Aggregate functions return many values while scalar functions return one value.
Aggregate functions return one value while scalar functions return many values.
Aggregate functions take one value while scalar functions take many values.

questions.question: The SQL function ABS takes a number and returns the absolute value of that number.\nTherefore, the ABS function is a(n) _____.
Scalar functions take one value and return at least one value.
In relational databases, all transaction must follow _____ principles.
Which of the following statements regarding database transactions is false?
options: Transactions are made up one operation.
Transactions represent a single unit of work in a database.
Transactions can only modify data in allowed ways.

questions.question: Transactions in relational databases are said to be ______, since they make a single unit\nof work.
Which T-SQL command is used to clear all work done by a transaction?
Which aggregate function is used to get the average value of a numeric column?

Below is a SQL query for the Person and Student tables:
SELECT p.Name, s.Grade
FROM Person AS p
INNER JOIN Student AS s ON p.Id = s.StudentId;
Which of the following best describes the query’s results?
options: The student with the highest grade
The name of every student with a grade greater than 3.0
The name and grade of every person
The name and grade of every person that is a student

questions.question: Which of the following SQL statements could be added to the end of the above query to\nsort the results by the person’s name?

Below is a SQL query for the Student tables:
SELECT s.Grade, COUNT(s.Grade)
FROM Student AS s
GROUP BY s.Grade;
Which of the following best describes the query’s results?
options: The names of all students, grouped by grade
The number of students with each grade
The number of students with the highest grade

questions.question: By default, the ORDER BY keyword sorts results in ascending order.
Below is a SQL query for the Student tables:\nDELETE FROM Student AS s WHERE s.HasGraduated = 1;\nWhat would the query do if the WHERE clause is removed?
options: Delete all students who have graduated.
Delete all students from the table.

questions.question: Which SQL operator returns the results of multiple SELECT statements, where each\nrecord is present in all the queried tables?
Unlike the UNION operator, every SELECT statement used with INTERSECT must\nhave the same number and type of columns selected.
Data that is in 3NF is said to be more ______ compared to data in 0NF.
Every student in a high school has a homeroom class, and each homeroom class can\ncontain multiple students. What type of relationship is being described?
Every student in a high school has a locker, and each locker belongs to only a single\nstudent. What type of relationship is being described?
